intro.title: The things I reach for.
intro.sub: Hover an object to see the story. Click to step inside.

camera.label: Photography
camera.blurb: I shoot the moments most people scroll past — the quiet in-between frames at events, on the street, and around Singapore. What started as documenting friends became a genuine obsession with light, timing, and composition.

watch.label: Watches
watch.blurb: Mechanical watches run in the family. I grew up around a small collection that got passed down and added to over three generations — each piece carrying a story about who wore it and why. I've been the one keeping the record.

cards.label: Card throwing
cards.blurb: I found card throwing down a late-night rabbit hole and never climbed back out. It's equal parts physics and patience — grip, spin, release — and there's a strange calm in drilling the same throw a hundred times until it finally bites the target.

namecard.label: The professional side
namecard.blurb: The other side of the table. This card takes you back to the professional portfolio — the engineering, the markets, the work.

photography.name: Reginald Tan
photography.bio: Singapore. Events, street, and the occasional portrait. Shot on whatever's in my bag.

entries.line2: My grandfather's first mechanical — a hand-wound dress watch bought with his first proper paycheck.
A steel diver that survived the shipyard, the sea, and two decades of being worn every single day.
The dress watch moves to my father. He wears it to his own first job — and starts the habit of keeping notes on each piece.
The first automatic joins the drawer. No more winding. The collection quietly doubles over the decade.
I inherit the diver. Scratched, honest, still ticking — and I fall for the mechanics of the thing.
I maintain them all now: service history, stories, who wore what. A small archive that keeps on running.

cards.title: Card Toss
cards.intro: Drag back on the card, aim, and release to throw. Land it in the target rings — bullseye scores highest. Ten cards. See how sharp your flick is.
